root_text.sn15.2:1.3: Pubbā koṭi na paññāyati avijjānīvaraṇānaṁ sattānaṁ taṇhāsaṁyojanānaṁ sandhāvataṁ saṁsarataṁ.
root_text.sn15.2:1.4: Seyyathāpi, bhikkhave, puriso imaṁ mahāpathaviṁ kolaṭṭhimattaṁ kolaṭṭhimattaṁ mattikāguḷikaṁ karitvā nikkhipeyya:
root_text.sn15.2:1.5: ‘ayaṁ me pitā, tassa me pitu ayaṁ pitā’ti, apariyādinnāva bhikkhave, tassa purisassa pitupitaro assu, athāyaṁ mahāpathavī parikkhayaṁ pariyādānaṁ gaccheyya.
root_text.sn15.2:1.8: Pubbā koṭi na paññāyati avijjānīvaraṇānaṁ sattānaṁ taṇhāsaṁyojanānaṁ sandhāvataṁ saṁsarataṁ.
root_text.sn15.2:1.9: Evaṁ dīgharattaṁ vo, bhikkhave, dukkhaṁ paccanubhūtaṁ tibbaṁ paccanubhūtaṁ byasanaṁ paccanubhūtaṁ, kaṭasī vaḍḍhitā.
root_text.sn15.2:1.10: Yāvañcidaṁ, bhikkhave, alameva sabbasaṅkhāresu nibbindituṁ, alaṁ virajjituṁ, alaṁ vimuccitun”ti.

translation_text.sn15.2:1.2: “Mendicants, this transmigration has no known beginning.
translation_text.sn15.2:1.3: No first point is found of sentient beings roaming and transmigrating, shrouded by ignorance and fettered by craving.
translation_text.sn15.2:1.4: Suppose a person was to make the whole earth into clay balls the size of jujube seeds. They’d lay them down, saying:
translation_text.sn15.2:1.5: ‘This is my father, this is my grandfather.’ The whole earth would run out before that person’s fathers and grandfathers.
translation_text.sn15.2:1.7: This transmigration has no known beginning.
translation_text.sn15.2:1.8: No first point is found of sentient beings roaming and transmigrating, shrouded by ignorance and fettered by craving.
translation_text.sn15.2:1.9: For such a long time you have undergone suffering, agony, and disaster, swelling the cemeteries.
translation_text.sn15.2:1.10: This is quite enough for you to become disillusioned, dispassionate, and freed regarding all conditions.”
